title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Cluster Case: K8s Gateway Listener Tls
summary: Platform and application teams must share external HTTP routing without annotation-specific coupling, cross-namespace privilege, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s gateway listener tls increment in Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior.
Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.instruction:

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior. A defensible solution applies K8s Gateway Listener Tls, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Gateway API routing and delegation policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-5
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: worked k8s gateway ingress routing tls 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A TLS Secret reference is permitted from any namespace that can create an HTTPRoute. This fails the K8s Gateway Listener Tls contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
